_publications._publicationTitle: Zebrafish neurofibromatosis type 1 genes have redundant functions in tumorigenesis and embryonic development
_publications._context: First description of nf1a and nf1b double null zebrafish. Characterizes nf1a and nf1b loss-of-function alleles (nf1aΔ5, nf1aΔ8, nf1aL1247X for nf1a; nf1b+10, nf1bΔ55 for nf1b) and demonstrates that compound homozygous nf1a-/-;nf1b-/- fish develop mpnst-like tumors, establishing functional redundancy between the two zebrafish nf1 paralogues.

_publications._publicationTitle: Inhibition of Cxcr4 chemokine receptor signaling improves habituation learning in a zebrafish model of Neurofibromatosis
_publications._context: RNA-seq study of nf1a-/-;nf1b-/- zebrafish larvae demonstrating that Cxcr4 inhibition rescues habituation learning deficits; used GEO GSE317922.

basicInfo.description: Double homozygous null zebrafish lacking both nf1a and nf1b paralogs. nf1a and nf1b are the two zebrafish orthologs of human NF1, which encodes the RAS-GAP neurofibromin. Single knockouts display mild phenotypes; compound homozygous double nulls develop MPNST-like tumors and behavioral deficits including impaired habituation learning. First fully characterized in Shin et al. 2012 Disease Models & Mechanisms using alleles nf1aΔ5/nf1aL1247X;nf1bΔ55/nf1b+10.
basicInfo.synonyms: nf1a-/-;nf1b-/- zebrafish; nf1 double null zebrafish

mutationTypes: Loss-of-function (null alleles for both nf1a and nf1b)
